items.q: Why do we add a leap day every four years?
items.correct: Because the year is about 365.24 days, so an extra day keeps the calendar aligned.
items.misconception: To make February longer for no reason.
items.random_wrong: Because the Earth slows down each year.
items.why: The quarter-day surplus each year is corrected by inserting an extra day roughly every four years.

items.q: Is a sidereal day the same as a solar day?
items.correct: No - a sidereal day (relative to the stars) is about four minutes shorter.
items.random_wrong: A sidereal day is exactly twice as long.
items.why: Earth's orbital motion means it must turn slightly more than one rotation to face the Sun again.

items.correct: The Earth rotating on its axis.
items.misconception: The Earth moving around the Sun.
items.random_wrong: The Sun switching off and on.
items.why: One rotation gives one day-night cycle; the orbit around the Sun gives the year, not the day.
The answer examiners want is: 24 hours. “23 hours 56 minutes” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Why is a sidereal day shorter than a solar day?
items.correct: Earth must turn a little extra to face the Sun again
items.why: It's Earth must turn a little extra to face the Sun again. “Earth speeds up at night” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: A leap day is added because the tropical year is about:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 365.24 days. “Exactly 365 days” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Time zones are measured relative to:
items.why: It's Greenwich Mean Time (GMT). “The Equator” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Approximately how many days are in a tropical year?
items.why: Exact answer: 365.24 days. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
